commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The PR needs author code fixes, contributor real behavior proof, and maintainer sign-off on config/privacy/auth-provider boundaries before it is a safe merge candidate.; Needs attention: The diff introduces a transcript-to-model background path and currently sends transcript text for sessions whose generated title is unused.; Review finding: [P1] Use the full session model for title generation — src/auto-repl... (sha=46a6b78b3d651cdf772307e4b93c6ac1ae1fd618)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds opt-in AI session title generation, persists `autoTitle`, projects derived titles into session lists and the Control UI, and threads session model/auth profile choices into background title summarization.

PR surface: Source +340, Tests +249. Total +589 across 24 files.

Reproducibility: yes. From source, a session entry with `modelProvider` and `model` but no `modelOverride` reaches the title generator with no `modelId`, and a `displayName` or `subject` entry passes the only `autoTitle` guard before the model call.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs author code fixes, contributor real behavior proof, and maintainer sign-off on config/privacy/auth-provider boundaries before it is a safe merge candidate.

target.title: fix(sessions): auto-generate session titles via AI summarization
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: The session-title transcript scanner read only one 8192-byte chunk before deciding whether enough user turns existed, so a long first JSONL user record could be parsed as a truncated line and skipped.
- Solution: Stream complete transcript JSONL records in order and stop only after the configured number of user turns has been counted.
- What changed: Reused the existing transcript line streaming helper in `session-title-generator`, added a regression test with a long first user record followed by enough later user turns, and aligned current-main Control UI session refresh tests with the derived-title request path.
- What did NOT change: `sessionTitle.enabled` remains explicit opt-in. Manual session names and subject labels still take precedence over `autoTitle`. No dependency or lockfile changes remain in the PR range.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** Power users with long first prompts or tool-heavy opening turns can still get useful session titles once the configured turn threshold is reached, instead of keeping an unhelpful first-message fallback title.

Fixes #77165

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Session title generation must not miss the configured turn threshold when the first user JSONL record is longer than the old fixed read buffer. The PR also preserves the existing opt-in title behavior, auth-profile routing coverage, `autoTitle` persistence, and sidebar/session-list title precedence tests.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux x86_64, Node v22.22.0, current OpenClaw PR worktree after rebase onto current `origin/main`. Gateway startup used the daily-fix dev gateway script with an isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME` and alternate local port 19021 because port 19001 was already occupied locally.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
OPENCLAW_REPO=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-87643 \
  OPENCLAW_LOG=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-87643-evidence/openclaw-dev-gateway-round3-port19021.log \
  OPENCLAW_DEV_AUTO_PAIR=false \
  OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=19021 \
  OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=proof-token-87643 \
  /home/0668001030/.claude/skills/daily-fix/scripts/start_openclaw_dev_gateway.sh

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/auto-reply/reply/session-title-generator.test.ts \
  ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts

pnpm check:test-types
pnpm lint --threads=8
git diff --check
```
- **Evidence after fix:**
```text
2026-06-02T18:53:46.585+08:00 [gateway] agent model: zte/nebulacoder-cot-v8.0 (thinking=off, fast=off)
2026-06-02T18:53:46.588+08:00 [gateway] http server listening (8 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 3.3s)
2026-06-02T18:53:46.595+08:00 [gateway] ready
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  3 passed (3)
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  77 passed (77)
$ pnpm tsgo:test
$ pnpm tsgo:core:test && pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
$ node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
[oxlint:core] finished
[oxlint:extensions] finished
[oxlint:scripts] finished
```
- **Observed result after fix:** The long-first-record regression test writes a first user JSONL record over 8192 bytes, then two later user turns. On current head, title generation still calls the label generator with all three user messages and persists `autoTitle`, proving the scanner reads complete records and reaches the configured threshold. The current Control UI selected-agent session refresh tests, test typecheck, full lint, and diff whitespace check passed. The PR range no longer contains shrinkwrap changes.
- **What was not tested:** No live Control UI/Mantis screenshot was captured. The proof verifies the Control UI data path through `sessions.list` controller tests rather than a browser screenshot.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix

## Review findings addressed

- RF-002 / RF-008: Fixed the bounded JSONL scanner by reading complete transcript lines through the existing streaming transcript helper before checking the threshold.
- RF-003: Added a regression test for a long first user record followed by enough later user turns.
- RF-004 / RF-005 / RF-006 / RF-007: The config, privacy, and auth-provider boundaries are still maintainer decisions; this update keeps the feature default-off and preserves the existing focused auth/profile and precedence tests.
- RF-009 / RF-010: Re-ran the requested focused session-title and current Control UI session controller tests, plus test typecheck, full lint, and gateway startup on the rebased branch.
- RF-001: The PR was still labeled `status: ⏳ waiting on author` before this update; this commit addresses the author-side scanner finding so the label can be re-evaluated by the normal review flow.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility`, `merge-risk: 🚨 auth-provider`, `merge-risk: 🚨 security-boundary`, plus session-state and availability/message-delivery surfaces in the diff.
- **Risk explanation:** The broader PR adds a default-off `sessionTitle` config surface, durable `autoTitle` session metadata, and session-scoped model/auth routing for the background title request. This update only changes how the title generator reads transcript records before the model call and updates tests that assert the Control UI requests derived titles; it does not widen when background title requests run or which auth profile/model they use.
- **Why acceptable:** Generation still requires `sessionTitle.enabled: true`, manual `displayName` and existing `subject` keep precedence over `autoTitle`, and the new scanner uses the repository's existing bounded transcript streaming helper rather than reading whole transcript files. Maintainers still own final sign-off for the config/privacy/auth boundary, and the PR body keeps the Mantis/Control UI visual proof gap explicit.

## Maintainer-ready confidence

- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** Medium-high for the scanner blocker, current-main CI repro fixes, and local runtime/test evidence; medium overall because the feature remains a large PR with config/privacy/auth-provider merge-risk labels and no live Control UI/Mantis screenshot.
- **Patch quality notes:** The latest update is narrow: title scanner logging/lint cleanup plus Control UI test expectations for selected-agent refresh paths. The production change replaces the fixed chunk parser with an existing complete-line transcript stream. The fire-and-forget catch behavior, no-op default returns, and session-state updates are part of the existing PR design: failures do not block replies, missing config/session/transcript state leaves titles unchanged, and persisted `autoTitle` remains behind explicit opt-in generation.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Focused runtime startup, unit/regression tests, test typecheck, full lint, and diff whitespace check.
- Target test file: `src/auto-reply/reply/session-title-generator.test.ts`.
- Additional focused test files: `ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts`.
- Scenario locked in: A long first JSONL user record does not block counting later user turns; `sessionTitle` remains opt-in; selected-agent Control UI refresh calls request derived titles; `autoTitle` persists; subject/manual names remain stronger than `autoTitle`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: It covers the exact scanner defect at the title-generation boundary and the current-main CI expectations for the UI refresh path while preserving the existing tests for config, auth routing, persistence, and sidebar/session-list precedence.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The title generator treated a fixed-size byte chunk as if it were a complete JSONL prefix. That broke the transcript invariant that each JSONL line must be parsed only after the full record has been read, so a long first user message could be skipped and the later valid turns were not enough to trigger title generation.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The scanner now consumes the same complete-line transcript stream used elsewhere in session transcript handling. That moves the decision point from arbitrary byte boundaries to JSONL record boundaries, so threshold counting is based on real user records and the symptom cannot recur for long first records.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
